<Meta of={TableStories} title="Components/Table" />

# Table

Table displays a collection of data grouped into rows and columns.

Tables should only be used for tabular collections of interrelated data &mdash;
not for layout, nor for key-value pairs (use
[DescriptionList](/docs/components-descriptionlist--descriptionlist) for those).

Table is not a web component, but instead a set of CSS class(es) which you add onto the native
HTML elements such as `<table>`, `<tr>`, `<th>`, `<td>`, etc.

<Canvas of={TableStories.Primary} />

## Cell Alignment

You can align a table cell's text light, center, or right.

You can also add the "nowrap" option to a cell to prevents its contents
from line-wrapping.

<Canvas of={TableStories.CellAlignment} />

## Borders

By default, Table has cell borders, but you can use:

- the "borderless" option
- the "outer border" option, where there's just a border around the outside, but no borders between cells
- the "inner borders" option, where there are borders between cells, but no border around the outside

<Canvas of={TableStories.Borders} />

## Header Fill

You can give the table header a fill color.

<Canvas of={TableStories.HeaderFill} />

## Scroll Container

Tables cannot easily adapt to look good on smaller displays.
If your table is likely to be wider than the screen, wrap it in a table scroll container.
This will allow the user to scroll the table left
and right, and will add the necessary accessibility support.

Tables that use this feature can't use a `<caption>` tag inside the table, since the
caption would scroll with the
table. Instead, use an external heading with class
`atp-table-caption`, and give the table an `aria-describedby` attribute whose
value is the id of the external heading.

<Canvas of={TableStories.ScrollContainer} />

## "Description" Tables

This feature gives Tables the same visual look as
[Description List](/docs/components-descriptionlist--descriptionlist), so that the
two will harmonize when used next to each other.
It also provides a hover/focus
state for the rows in the table.

<Canvas of={TableStories.Description} />
